rulings.Case Title: JOHN JOHNSTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisifed its burden of production and plaintiff did not present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing terminal block attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objection to the declaration of Kenneth Cohen is sustained. The hearing on this motion will be at 10:45am, not 9:30am. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT NELSON et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Imo Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KIRAS et al VS. AMCORD, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Co.¿s motion for costs and fees and motion to preclude James Saunders from providing further testimony will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. = (220/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 20, 2011, line 2. Defendant The Dow Chemical Co.¿s demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s demurrer to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth and eighth causes of action alleged in plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained on both Workers¿ Compensation and product identification grounds. Plaintiffs are granted 10 days leave to amend to have the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to plead facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, and to plead facts sufficient to demonstrate that plaintiffs¿ claims against defendant do not fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the California Workers¿ Compensation Act. = (503/TLJ)
